Event Name/Title

Cultivating Ignorance

Event Date

22 Apr 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Choose an item.

Transcriber

Manoj Singh Kushwaha

Completed

25 July 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-043-Cultivating%20Ignorance%20(Mumbai)-BG_14_19-dt_90_04_22.mp3

Source Video

Verse

BG 14.19

Tags – Places

Vrindavan

Tags – Pastimes

Akshaya Patra

Tags – Persons

Krishna, Radha, Durga, Durvasa Muni, Gopis, Ambarish Maharaj, Pandavas, Duryodhana, Draupadi, Kunti Maharani

Tags – Themes

nanyam gunebhyah kartaram
yada drastanupasyati
gunebhyas ca param vetti
mad-bhavam so 'dhigacchati

[Bg. 14.19]

[When one properly sees that in all activities no other performer is at work than these modes of nature and he knows the Supreme Lord, who is transcendental to all these modes, he attains My spiritual nature.]

Purport

One can transcend all the activities of the modes of material nature, simply by understanding them properly by learning from the proper souls. The real spiritual master is Krishna, and He is imparting this spiritual knowledge to Arjuna. Similarly, it is from those who are fully in Krishna consciousness that one has to learn this science of activities in the terms of the modes of material nature. Otherwise, one's life will be misdirected. By the instruction of a bona fide spiritual master, a living entity can know his spiritual position, his material body, his senses, how he is entrapped, and how he is under the spell of the material modes of nature. He is helpless, being in the grip of these modes, but when he can see his real position, then he can attain the transcendental position, having the scope for spiritual life. Actually, the living entity is not the performer of different activities. He is forced to act because he is situated in a particular type of body, conducted by some particular mode of material nature. Unless one has the help of spiritual authority, he cannot understand in what position he actually is situated. With the association of a bona fide spiritual master, he can see his real position, and by such an understanding he can become fixed in full Krishna consciousness. A man in Krishna consciousness is not controlled by the spell of the material modes of nature. It has already been stated in the Seventh Chapter that one who has surrendered to Krishna is relieved from the activities of material nature. Therefore, for one who is able to see things as they are, the influence of material nature gradually ceases.

Lecture Transcription

Nature of this world

In the Bhagavad-gita, Lord Sri Krishna has said,

jnanam te 'ham sa-vijnanam
idam vaksyamy asesatah
yaj jnatva neha bhuyo 'nyaj
jnatavyam avasisyate

[Bg. 7.2]

That this knowledge is complete, because when you have thus learned this science, you will know both nominal and phenomenal. The nature of the temporary, as well as the nature of the eternal. You see the Bhagavad-gita is teaching us, how to live in this world but not of this world. But in order to do such we must not only understand what is the nature of the soul and what is the nature of God, but we must also understand, what is the nature of this world? This temporary world which is conducted by the three modes of material nature.

What is Ignorance?

It is described by Sri Krishna that this body is the field of activities, the Kshetra and soul is the Kshetrajna. He is the knower of the field of activities. And He explains that one must simultaneously understand the nature of the field of the activities as well as the nature of the soul, the nature of the performer of the activities. And Isoponishad further elaborates on this point by explaining that one must simultaneously cultivate knowledge and ignorance. What does that mean – “to cultivate Ignorance”? It means to understand what ignorance is. It does not mean you should become ignorant. Many people have done like this, they say that Isoponishad says you should cultivate ignorance, so that it means I should act very much having good time in the mode of ignorance, and in this way, I have to cultivate ignorance. We should understand the nature of the ignorance. What is ignorance and how it is keeping you bound to the concept of misery, the bodily concept of life.

Divine energy manifests in two forms

This material world is one of the divine energies of the Lord. It is described in the shastra that Sri Krishna expands Himself as Srimati Radharani, who is the divine energy of the Lord. Now in the spiritual world, Krishna also has a sister, who is an expansion of Sri Radharani. Her name is Subhadra. You have seen that at Ratha Yatra there is one cart of Jagannath, who is Krishna and His brother and sister also have their own chariots, which is Subhadra and Balaram ji. So, Subhadra, who is the personification of Yoga Maya, she incarnates herself into this world as Durga. Durga is the keeper of the prison of material existence. In fact, the Sanskrit word Durga means prison. Now is Durga material? Durga is not material. She is the spiritual energy of the Lord, but she is acting in such a way that she is revealing herself to those who are in ignorance.

There is only one energy, but that energy manifests itself differently according to the receptacle. Just like from the powerhouse, electricity is coming. It is the same current of electricity. It is not two types of electricity. But in one receptacle, a stove, the electricity manifests itself as extreme heat for cooking. And in another receptacle is the refrigerator, that same electricity is manifesting itself as freezing cold. Now is there a difference in electricity? It is the difference in who is receiving it or what is receiving the electricity. It is not that the electricity is hot or cold.

Similarly, in the state there is the police department and there is also so many social welfare departments. Now everyone likes the social welfare department, correct? Because they do good for everyone, whoever you are. At least, they are supposed to be doing good for everyone. But the police department, if you are a criminal, you will hate the police department. But if you are a good citizen, you like the police department. Of course, in Kali Yuga it is the opposite. If you are a good citizen, you are afraid of the police and if you are a crook, you like the police because usually they are working for you, in Kali Yuga. But in a natural condition, the police, they are both agencies of the government. Therefore, the Government sees that they are both good. But the criminal sees one is bad. Similarly, there is the prison house. Prison house is also run by the government. Now for a criminal, the prison house is a very bad thing. But for the good citizens, the prison house is a very good thing. So similarly, this material energy is essentially spiritual. It is God’s energy and nothing about God is temporary. Nothing about God is material. Everything emanating from God is pure and spiritual.

oṁ pūrṇam adaḥ pūrṇam idaṁ
pūrṇāt pūrṇam udacyate
pūrṇasya pūrṇam ādāya
pūrṇam evāvaśiṣyate

[Isopanisad invocation]

[The Personality of Godhead is perfect and complete, and because He is completely perfect, all emanations from Him, such as this phenomenal world, are perfectly equipped as complete wholes. Whatever is produced of the Complete Whole is also complete in itself. Because He is the Complete Whole, even though so many complete units emanate from Him, He remains the complete balance.]

That the absolute truth is perfect and complete and everything emanating from Him is perfect and complete including this temporary material energy. It is perfect and complete because it is coming from Krishna. Can anything imperfect come from the perfect? Not possible. It is illogical. But if our desire is not in harmony with the desire of God, then the beautiful form of Srimati Radharani, oftentimes she is offering flowers to Gopinath and at other time she is holding her hand out to bless her devotee. Laxmidevi is always holding her hand out to bless her devotee.

Three-fold miseries

But Durga Devi, she does not have her hand out like this. She has a trident. She usually has ten hands with swords,

and clubs, and all sorts of very violent looking weapons. And there is the famous picture of Durga where there is a demon and the lion, he has a very firm grip on the demon and she is striking the trident within his chest, right in his heart. The trident indicates the three modes…. the three-fold miseries of material existence, adhyatmika, adhibhautika, and adhidaivika. Miseries caused by one’s own body and mind, miseries caused by other living beings and miseries caused by natural disturbances.

Miseries caused by one’s mind/body

Now in this world we utilize our good human intelligence to try to somehow or the other counteract the three-fold miseries of material existence. Just like miseries caused by one’s own body - disease. People are getting diseases like anything and therefore we have so many doctors and so many hospitals trying to somehow or other relieve the distress of disease. Sometimes doctors complicate the disease. Sometimes it is something so simple, and they become so complicated, when we go to the doctor.

In the Vedic system, in the old days, the doctors were brahmanas and they would never ask for money. They would simply offer their services freely for the benefit of others. But today it’s a very very good profession and people are not so concerned with people anymore; they are concerned with money. This is unfortunate. This is the disease - Greed. The disease of greed is worse than the disease of disease. Why? Because it’s the greed of disease… it’s the disease of greed that makes you commit sinful activities, and the reaction is a disease. That’s why we say, “Physician, heal thyself”.

So of course, we have many many hospitals, and we have such a great great scientific endeavor to cure the various diseases of the world. But we find that no matter how great medical technology becomes, there is still disease. Just like at one time there was in Europe, there was the bubonic fever, what is called black plague. Practically half the population of Europe was killed. This was only a few hundred years ago. People think it can never happen to us. And then they found some cure. Then they are thinking, yeah very nice. Then there was Tuberculosis. So many people were killed and then they found a cure. And then cancer comes. Then they are studying to find some cures. But then AIDS comes, now they do not know what to do? Someday they may find a cure. But some other disease will come. Throughout history there is always a disease that cannot be cured. Has there ever been a time in modern history that there was not a disease that at that time was not curable. What do you say, doctors? Always like that. Why? Because if your Karma is to die of a disease that’s incurable, yes you can cure it. But Krishna just sends another one.

Physical v/s mental distress

So, there is disease caused by the body and even if you are very healthy, still you must grow old and you must die. It is a miserable condition. So, one point of Durga’s trident is the misery caused by your own body and mind. Mental disease is even worse than physical disease. Generally, no matter how much physical distress a person’s in, he somehow or the other tolerates it. But when mental distress gets too much, they jump off buildings. How many people commit suicide due to physical distress? Very few. But very many due to mental distress. When the ghost of anxiety just starts usurping your mind, it is very frustrating. Horrible it's worse than the physical disease. You can take some pain reliever and you can somehow or other cope with physical disease. But due to mental disease people are taking drugs and taking intoxication of alcohol, simply because they are mentally so frustrated and bored with life. They have to find some relief, some way to dull their minds. And the greatest problem in America now is not AIDS but the greatest problem in America is drugs, narcotics destroying the whole country. The streets are practically battlefields. People are being killed every day like anything, in the wars over who gets the land to sell the drugs. Young people are being given drugs and the first time you use it practically you are addicted. And this is considered socially very very distinguished to take drugs. In fact if you do not take it you are an outcast in many places. Even when I was a child, I remember, drugs come in,

“You do not take this drug, ha ha! What are you? From some other planets?”

This is what is happening; This is the ‘in’ thing and the young people, they want to be ‘in’. They do not want to be out. People look at them, like,

“We do not want to associate with you, you are out.”

“Okay, I’ll take it. I’m in, I’m in.”

“Alright. You are in. Ha Ha!”

Yes. This is what is happening. This is happening more and more in India. To the degree India becomes westernized, to that degree there will be drug addicts. And there will be AIDS and so many other nice adhyatmika diseases, sufferings, miseries caused by one’s body and one’s mind.

Miseries caused by other living entities.

And there is also miseries caused by other living beings. Just like sometimes we become bitten by mosquitoes. It is very frustrating. Sometimes we get bitten by snakes, that is even more frustrating. Sometimes we get eaten by lions and tigers, that is even more frustrating. Sometimes we are insulted by human animals which is very very frustrating. Sometimes even our own loved ones, they turn against us and cause us great pain and anxiety. Sometimes there is terrorism and wars. This is pain, suffering caused by other living beings.[18:42]

Miseries caused by natural disturbances.

There is also adhibhautika, miseries caused by natural disturbances - too much heat, too much cold. Just like I remember a few years ago we took about 50 devotees to the Himalayas for a pilgrimage. We went in the month of May and we were in Delhi. It was very hot, so hot, and it was miserably hot. Anyone who thought that they were this material body were suffering like anything. It was very very hot that year. You remember? People were just, “Let me sleep until the sun goes down,” just to forget the pain. So, everyone was thinking, “Yes, we are going to the Himalayas, there it will be nice, cool and refreshing. You remember this trip? So, we went to the Himalayas. Then it started becoming nice, cool and refreshing. Everyone was thinking yes, there is relief. We were thinking we were going to a pilgrimage but actually it’s a nice vacation from the heat.

Then we got to Badrinatha, Badirkashram. When we were there, we were all enjoying the nice cool refreshing atmosphere and we said, “Let us chant and dance through the roads of Badrinatha.” So, we started chanting and the demigods must have been very pleased because all of sudden big big white flowers started showering from the sky. But these flowers were very cold. In America we call it snow. And it was so much snow. That it was very deep, and it was very cold and there was no sun to be seen anywhere. The sky became grey, almost black and it was freezing, and of course no one took any warm clothes because we all were thinking it will be nice & refreshing. And it was so cold. We were thinking, “Let’s go, enough of this pilgrimage, let’s go.” But the roads were blocked. Because there was too much snow. The military were standing with their guns saying, “You cannot go. You must stay.” We all were trembling, freezing. There were No blankets. We were thinking, how nice it was in Delhi. Delhi was so nice and warm.

Modes of nature do not discriminate.

The grass is always greener on the other side. Whatever misery we are in now, understand, tomorrow we are going to think how nice it was, to be like this. So, there was no escape. We were trapped. I will not mention names, but we had people who were very very big controllers of companies, millionaires. They just tell their servants, “Do this, do this.” The servants would be like – “Yes sir, yes sir!” But now even such people, with all the money in the whole world, they could not get one blanket. If they would have sold their whole industry, they could not get down that road. Everyone was equal. There was no question of whether you are educated or uneducated, whether you are a PhD or whether you are illiterate, whether you are a mataji or whether you are a sanyasi. You are cold.

When the modes of material nature strike, they do not discriminate what your position is. Similarly, at the time of death we should know that we are all stripped naked with nothing and we are just standing before God. And all we have is our faith and our devotion. None of our material acquisitions in this world will be of any avail.

So, who causes the heat and the cold? Sometimes there is drought. Sometimes there is too much rain and floods. Just too much rain, everything is destroyed, floods. If there is not enough water everything is destroyed, draught. It is described that the demigods, the Devatas, the higher powers, who are petitioned by Lord Sri Krishna to manage the affairs of the universe. They are the ones, these higher powers, who are creating the miseries in the pleasures of material life, on the platform of Adhibhautika - miseries caused by natural disturbances.

One who is in knowledge no longer suffers.

So, you see for one who is in ignorance, that person is always being stabbed by the trident of Durga. But for one who is in knowledge, that person sees that actually this so-called pleasure and pain, honour and dishonour, happiness and distress; They are all the temporary manifestations of Maya. But maya is Krishna’s divine spiritual energy. The source of Maya is Yogamaya, Laxmidevi, Sri Radharani. She is always standing before us offering her benediction. So therefore when we know: mayadhyaksena prakrtih suyate sa-caracaram [BG 9.10]. That Krishna, as he says in Bhagavad-gita, he is directing this material nature. He is the master and lord of this material nature. When we understand how He is transcendental and how we are a part of Him and therefore we are also thoroughly transcendental. And how He creates this prison house of material nature which causes simply an opportunity for rebellious living entities to realize the suffering condition of being apart from Krishna. Then we no longer suffer the effects of the material existence.

We see the divine hand of God behind every situation of life. Krishna explains in the Bhagavad-gita how the modes of material nature are working. How they are affecting the body and how they are affecting the mind. Unless we understand exactly how these things are happening, we will be bewildered.

prakrteh kriyamanani
gunaih karmani sarvasah
ahankara-vimudhatma
kartaham iti manyate

[BG 3.27]

[The spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature.]

The bewildered spirit soul thinks he is the doer of activities but actually they are all being carried out by the three modes of nature - the mode of goodness (satva guna), the mode of passion (rajo guna), and mode of ignorance (tamo guna). When we understand how these three modes are working, then we can learn how to be Krishna Conscious, we can learn how to take shelter of Krishna, we can learn how to remember Krishna, we can learn how to be transcendental even in the face of three modes of material nature.

Maya constantly tests us.

We will constantly be tested by Maya. Now for a materialistic person who is in ignorance, those tests are simply nothing but punishment and pain. But for a devotee, when Maya tests, he sees that this is the most blessed opportunity for me to purify my consciousness. And the fact is, that is what Maya is for, to help you in your Krishna Consciousness. That is why queen Kunti, she prayed in such a way, very few people dare to pray in the same way as queen Kunti.

vipadaḥ santu tāḥ śaśvat
tatra tatra jagad-guro
bhavato darśanaṁ yat syād
apunar bhava-darśanam

[SB 1.8.25]

[I wish that all those calamities would happen again and again so that we could see You again and again, for seeing You means that we will no longer see repeated births and deaths.]

She says that “Krishna, let the miseries, the sufferings, the calamities, come to me again and again and again because, in the face of these conditions of life, I remember you and in remembering you, I never have to see birth and death.”

Calamities are opportunities to help increase our surrender.

It is said that the death personified, and fear personified are afraid of Krishna. When we remember Krishna there is no more death. There is no more misery. Any more than in the presence of the Sun, there can be darkness. So, some of us think that I chant, I remember Krishna; but still, I am suffering too much. Why? Because we have to learn how to remember Krishna in the same feeling as queen Kunti. We have to surrender our minds, surrender our consciousness to the feet of Krishna, to really remember Him. We have to learn to take shelter in Him. And generally, when everything is very nice, our religion, our spiritual life, is just a formality, or a hobby; you use that word in India, it’s a part of something that we do, it is a nice part of our life. It gives peace to our mind. That is alright to have peace in the mind, is alright. But a little peace of mind is not going to help you from the tragedies of your life. When the real tragedies of life come, there is only one salvation and that is totally taking utter shelter at the feet of God. People are very fond of being religious. I will perform my pious activities.

Religion culminates in surrender.

Just like yesterday someone was telling me that he knows one man, he is a very religious man. He goes to the holy places, but he takes a little alcohol, whenever it is required. So, this man is very religious. He is very pious, and he is a good man. But still, he takes alcohol. So, he is saying, what is wrong with that? I was explaining that he may be a little religious but Krishna in Bhagavad-gita, He says:

sarva-dharman parityajya
mam ekam saranam vraja
aham tvam sarva-papebhyo
moksayisyami ma sucah

[BG 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. If you are religious, that is very good. You will gradually make advancement. But ultimately you have to come to the point of surrender. If you want to become freed from the repetition of birth and death. Being religious will not bring you to that point of - no more death or no more suffering. Only surrender, surrender to the supreme will of God. Now the question is - does God want you to drink alcohol? If he does not, then you may be religious but there is a defect, there is a flaw in your religion. But if you actually want to become freed from the sufferings of material existence, you have to surrender, and surrender means to do the will of God.

This person says, but this man loves God. I said, “Love? What kind of love? If your beloved tells you he does not like something, then you will die before doing that because you know, this will displease him or hurt him.” Krishna feels pain when we engage in sinful activities. Because He loves us. Just like a father, when he sees the child behaving in such a way that the child is going to suffer, it pains the father, out of love. So, if you love Krishna you will not want to give Him any pain. If you love your Guru, you will not want to give him any pain and every time we deviate we cause pain. Whether you are a devotee or non-devotee, you are still Krishna’s son and you are still causing him pain.

So, religion culminates in surrender. And Queen Kunti is talking about this sweet feeling of surrender. That when everything is nice, “Krishna now everything is too nice. Now I am the queen of Dwarka, not Dwarka, Hastinapur. Now I am the queen of Hastinapur. Now we have conquered the whole world. Now we are in a very pleasant condition. But we were safe when we were living in the jungles with nothing, because we had no other shelter except You. Now we have so many shelters. Now it is easy to forget You.”

Durvasa Muni blesses Radharani

There is a story in the Shastra. It describes how Durvasa Muni, who was a very great yogi, he was a partial expansion of Lord Shiva. Lord Shiva is Ashutosha, which means he is very easily pleased, and he is very easily angry. He gives very quick benedictions, but he also gives very quick curses with his anger. Just like it is described that, one-time Lord Shiva came to Vrindavan. Gopis are very simple cowherd girls, and when Lord Shiva came to Vrindavan, he had serpents around his neck and crematorium ashes, so they became very frightened. That was not Shiva, that was Durvasa Muni, I am sorry. Durvasa Muni is an expansion of Lord Shiva, when he came to Vrindavana, he looked very severe tapasvi with matted hair and he was very much like a yogi from the Himalayas. So, the gopis became afraid of him, so they ran away. But Srimati Radharani, she was thinking, he is a guest, we must treat him nicely. So, with very sweet words she greeted him, and she gave him nice Prasad and she just was so nice to him. Durvasa muni was so pleased, he gave her a benediction. He said, “Whatever you cook, will be sweeter than nectar and anyone who eats your cooking will never fall sick.” So, when mother Yashoda found out about this benediction given to Sri Radharani, she asked her to cook for Krishna everyday so he would never become sick. So in this way, Durvasa Muni sometimes gives nice benediction.

Durvasa Muni visits the Pandavas in the forest.

But you know in the story of Ambarish maharaja, when Druvasa Muni was angry he created a fire demon to kill him but Ambarish was saved by the will of the God. So, one day Durvasa Muni came to the house of Duryodhana and Duryodhana pleased Durvasa Muni very nicely. See, Duryodhana was very clever, he knew, “If I really really go out of my way to please Durvasa Muni he will give me a benediction and just see what I do with the benediction.” So, he satisfied Durvasa Muni and Durvasa Muni said, “Ah, Duryodhana you have made me very happy”. You have gone out of your way with your hospitality. Now what benediction can I give you?” Duryodhana said, my cousins, the Pandavas, I think you should go to their house for Prasad. That is the benediction I want. Go to their house for Prasad and take all your followers with you.

Durvasa muni had 10,000 disciples and he would always travel with many many disciples. Now Duryodhana told Durvasa that you should go there exactly at this time of the day, late in the afternoon. So, he went with his thousands of disciples and they arrived. Draupadi was there with the Pandavas. At that time, they were living in exile. They were in the forest in a little cottage. Duryodhana was not satisfied that they were banished from the kingdom and living in the jungle. He wanted to see them get cursed. He knew that if they could not feed Durvasa and all his disciples nicely, Durvasa would curse them because he would be very angry.

So Durvasa Muni arrived and Yudhisthira Maharaja greeted him. Durvasa Muni said, “We are very hungry. We have come here for lunch.” Yudhisthira Maharaja was thinking, “Oh, my God. We have nothing, we are living in the jungles. It is not that we have any servants or grocery stores. What do we have?” He said, “Why don’t you go to take your bath in the river? We will prepare food.” Durvasa Muni said, “Alright, but I’ll be back and we are very hungry.” So, they went to the river to take a bath. At that time Maharaja Yudhisthira went to Draupadi and said, “We have to prepare something for thousands and thousands of people, otherwise we will be cursed for sure.” Now Draupadi had a special plate, and this plate was given to her by Surya Deva - the Sun God and it is said, that plate would give as much food as was required. If a million people came for Prasad to her house, that plate would produce instantly enough for a million people. But there was one limitation, that it only had this power until she ate. After she completed her Prasad then it became an ordinary plate till the next day. So, she had just completed her prasad. Because Duryodhana knew what exactly the time she would eat and sent them just at that time. So, she was in great distress, “Now my plate is ordinary, we do not have any food, nothing, in our house, not even a grain. What do we do? We are going to get cursed. We are going to displease this yogi.” So Draupadi began to cry and of course, in this condition, she was crying out to Krishna. “Krishna save me. We have no one to turn to now. We are powerless. Save us Krishna. Please save us!!”

Krishna is in everyone’s heart. He hears your every prayer, He hears your every word, whether you like it or not. You can be assured 100% that whenever you pray to Krishna, He hears that prayer. And He will respond according to what is best for you. But you can also be assured. When you talk all nonsense, he hears that, as well. So, Krishna heard the heartfelt prayer of Draupadi and He just happened to walk by. It was in the middle of the forest. It was in the jungles and in the wilderness. But Krishna just happened to be walking by that day. And He came to the house of Draupadi and He said, “Draupadi, I am very hungry.” She just put her head down and she started to cry because after all she was a very soft-hearted lady. She started to cry. “Krishna! What to do? I don’t… Getting cursed by Durvasa Muni that is very bad. But, to not have anything for you, the supreme lord of our life, when You come to our house & this is the worst thing that’s ever happened. Getting cursed, we are afraid of, but displeasing you, that is worse than death.” She was just crying and crying. “Krishna, please forgive me. Please help me.” And Krishna, seeing how she was so sincerely taking shelter of Him, He said, “Just give me something, anything.” She said, “There is nothing.” He said, “Go down and look in the pot, just bring the pot that you cooked in today.” So, she brought the pot and there was just the tiny little shred of a vegetable stuck to the bottom of the pot. And Krishna took it and ate it. And He said, “Thank you very much. I am very satisfied.”

Now, when you water the root of the tree, every part of the tree is satisfied. Krishna is the root. He is the source of everything. When Krishna is satisfied, your life is perfect. If you satisfy everyone else but Krishna is not satisfied, then your life is a useless waste. So, Durvasa Muni and all of his disciples, sitting in the river, taking their bath, all of sudden they became very filled up, just as if they had eaten a huge feast. They were like, you know how you are when you are very full, they were like that. Then Draupadi, knowing what Krishna had done, she told Bhima, “You go call them all to have Prasad.” So, Bhima went to the river and with his very loud courageous voice, he said, “Durvasa Muni, maharaja and all your disciples please come, we have prepared a nice feast for you.” They looked at each other thinking, “My God, you know what Maharaja Yudhisthira, he prepares a feast that is such an opulent and luxurious feast and if we do not eat it, will be such an offence and we cannot even take one spoon.” So, they all just waited for Bhima to turn his back and then they all ran away, very secretly, just ran into the jungles and disappeared. In this way Krishna saved them.

Devotee’s take on reversals in life.

So, this is the process of Krishna Consciousness, that whether you like it or do not like it, the nature of this material world, due to its temporary conditioning, is that there is going to be reversals and sufferings and tests in our life. And those tests which are brought about by the power of Krishna through Maya, we should know that these tests are the greatest opportunity for me to take shelter of the Supreme Lord. To remember the words of mother Kunti. But if we do not understand the principles of how this material nature is working and why it is working the way it is? If we do not understand this, we will simply become bewildered, endlessly bewildered, by the endlessly transmutable changes of this world. Why is this happening to me? How many of us ask this question? Raise your hand if you never ask this question. Raise your hand if you sometimes ask this question. Why is this happening to me? What have I done? I am such a nice person. I may have made some mistakes, but I do not deserve this. We all ask this question. It is the universal question. Whether you are a black, white, Indian, Asian, American, African, Hindu, Christian, Muslim, Jews, young, or old. Everyone asks this question. Why is this happening to me? A devotee does not ask this question. A devotee says, “Krishna, you are very kind.”

So, you see, the scientists, they are trying to figure out how this world is working, correct. But no matter, how many experiments they perform and no matter how many theories they present to the world, they still cannot figure out how this world is working. They may not figure out how one little aspect is working. But a devotee, not only does he understand, how this world is working; But he understands a question, which is millions and millions and millions of times more important - Why is this world working the way it is? Do the material scientists ever ask that question? They ask ‘how’ but they never ask ‘why’? But Bhagavad-gita teaches us not only how, but also why? And when we understand this one verse:

mayadhyaksena prakrtih
suyate sa-caracaram
hetunanena kaunteya
jagad viparivartate

[BG 9.10]

[This material nature, which is one of My energies, is working under My direction, O son of Kuntī, producing all moving and nonmoving beings. Under its rule this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again.]

That Krishna is directing everything that is existing within this material world. It is all happening by His will. Not a blade of grass can move without His sanction.

Today’s verse, when you see that there is nothing beyond these modes of nature in all activities and that the supreme lord is transcendental to all those modes, then you can know My spiritual nature. So, let us become like Draupadi and her mother-in-law Kunti, in this way. In the face of great reversals, they will come into your life, no matter who you are. In the face of great testing, we should simply take the opportunity to understand that the nature of this material existence, it is like an ocean, I cannot cross it. But if I simply take shelter of Krishna, then this ocean becomes reduced to the size of the hoof print of a calf - the water contained therein. This is a verse from the Bhagavatam.

Every opportunity – good or bad, be grateful!

Every opportunity in our life, to take shelter of Krishna, we should be thankful, and we should be grateful. We should not complain, we should not try to put the blame on others. How many are like this? When things go completely against our will, we like to blame others. It is all his fault. If only he did like this then it would be. How many do like this, raise your hand? How many never blame anybody else? We always like to blame others. We always like to think how this is unfair. We always like to criticize. We always like to find faults. What we are doing is, we are escaping the opportunity to surrender to Krishna. We are escaping the opportunity to take shelter of the Lord. Therefore, we should have this faith, that Krishna is non-different than his name. And if we simply learn to take shelter of Krishna’s name in all circumstances of life - good or bad, pleasurable or painful, we never have to see the trident of Durga. We will always see the blessed form of God. We will always see the sweet and beautiful face of Krishna in the background behind everything that is happening in our life. If we simply learn how to take shelter of his name, we will never be bewildered, we will never be confused, we will never be in this nonsense mentality, why is this happening to me? Rather we will always be like, “Krishna, thank you! You are very kind.”

“Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare/Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare”.

[devotees:] “Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare/Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare”.

Shelter of the holy name

Our spiritual master used to tell us that if you simply have complete faith that Krishna is non-different than His name then you are liberated from all the miseries of material life. So therefore, it is very important to come to the temples, to come before the beautiful form of the Lord, to associate with the Lord’s devotees, to hear the philosophy of life being presented according to the authorized disciplic lines coming from Krishna. By hearing the glories of the holy name, by understanding how this nature is working and why? This whole material world in Kali Yuga is created just to get you to take shelter of Krishna’s name. If you see it as that, then you welcome everything and you are happy and peaceful, even in the face of most unbearable conditions of life. But if you forget that, then you are simply a victim of Kali Yuga. You will be bewildered, perplexed, disheartened. So, let us learn how to take shelter of Krishna’s names. Let us see His loving hand in our life.

“Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare/Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare.”

devotees:] “Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare/Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare”.

There is a famous song in America, this was back in the 60s. A devotee can take gold even from a filthy place. The name of the song was, “You cannot always get what you want. But if you try sometimes you just might find, you will get what you need.” Now of course he was talking about something completely different. But a devotee can take gold even from a filthy place. But this is our philosophy, you cannot always get what you want. But you should know that you are always getting what you need. So, I should be just dissatisfied. Krishna is all knowing and all loving and He is our friend. suhṛdaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ [BG 5.29] [He is the best friend of everyone.]

Just have faith, that Krishna is our friend, that He is the master and controller and He is non-different than His holy name, to turn to, in all circumstances of life. And then we can learn what it really means “To chant Hare Krishna and be Happy”.

Is there any question?

End of the transcription.

Q&A Transcription

[devotees:] Maharaja you said that Kunti maharani is praying that we must get more and more suffering so that we can be more and more Krishna conscious, but it has been observed that for neophyte devotee, whatever difficulties they are getting, they develop hatred against Krishna, at that point he has not reached to that level, whatever is coming, it is coming from Krishna, that time he thinks, why is it me only? In that circumstance, what a devotee must do?

[Maharaja:] You must associate with people on that level and then he will be given proper intelligence. It is very simple. That is why the association of devotees is very important in our life. They will remind us, they will guide us. But we have to open our hearts to hear.
